QF.id: XIII Trading, Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_14

question.text: What happens when open interest in a stock reaches 95% of the MWPL in equity derivatives?

answer.text: The stock enters a 'ban period' where no new positions can be created — only existing positions can be squared off or closed
Trading in all derivatives of that stock is halted until OI falls below 80%
The margin requirement doubles for all positions in that stock
Only institutional investors are allowed to trade; retail investors are banned

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When aggregate OI across exchanges reaches 95% of MWPL, the exchange bans fresh positions in all derivative contracts on that stock. Traders can only reduce or close existing positions. The ban is lifted when OI falls below 80% of MWPL on a sustained basis. **The Trap:** Trading is NOT completely halted — square-offs are allowed. It's not just margin increase; it's a position creation ban. The ban applies to ALL participants, not selectively. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch7 covers MWPL ban periods. This is unique to equity derivatives — currency and interest rate derivatives don't have an equivalent mechanism. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If your stock enters the ban period and you hold positions, you can exit but can't add. Also, a penalty is levied on any unhedged open positions during the ban period.
